message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker state

Update beads issue tracker JSONL to reflect current task progress
after typed client integration tests and f64 assertion fixes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1ey4","title":"Add wa robot workflow abort command","description":"# wa robot workflow abort

## Current State (CLI Stub Done)
✅ CLI subcommand structure added
✅ JSON schema created (wa-robot-workflow-abort.json)
❌ Implementation returns not-implemented error

## What Remains
Implement workflow abort with cleanup and audit trail.

## Implementation
1. Accept execution_id and optional --reason
2. Validate workflow is abortable (not already completed)
3. Mark as aborting, run cleanup steps
4. Release pane lock
5. Record in audit trail

## Testing Requirements
- Unit: Abort stops running workflow
- Unit: Cleanup steps run (unless --force)
- Unit: Pane lock released
- Unit: Error code stability (E_ALREADY_COMPLETED, etc.)
- E2E: See bd-qvbz for lifecycle scenario

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Safely aborts running workflows
- [ ] Cleanup steps execute (unless --force)
- [ ] Pane lock released after abort
- [ ] Audit trail records abort with reason
- [ ] JSON validates against schema
- [ ] Unit + E2E tests pass with detailed logging

Related: wa-55y (parallel implementation track)

 {"id":"bd-1ez8","title":"Set up automated nightly rebase CI for WezTerm fork","description":"## Overview

WezTerm is updated nightly by its author. To maintain our feature branch, we need automated rebasing with conflict detection and alerting.

## Prerequisites

- Completed: bd-20fw (WezTerm fork with wa integration)

## CI Workflow Design

### GitHub Actions Workflow

\`\`\`.github/workflows/rebase-upstream.yml\`\`\`
\`\`\`yaml
name: Rebase on Upstream WezTerm

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 6 * * *'  # Daily at 6 AM UTC
  workflow_dispatch:      # Manual trigger

jobs:
  rebase:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          ref: feature/wa-integration
          token: \${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "wa-bot"
          git config user.email "wa-bot@example.com"
      
      - name: Add Upstream Remote
        run: |
          git remote add upstream https://github.com/wez/wezterm.git
          git fetch upstream main
      
      - name: Attempt Rebase
        id: rebase
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          git rebase upstream/main
          echo "rebase_status=\$?" >> \$GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Push if Successful
        if: steps.rebase.outcome == 'success'
        run: |
          git push --force-with-lease origin feature/wa-integration
      
      - name: Create Conflict Issue
        if: steps.rebase.outcome == 'failure'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const { data: issues } = await github.rest.issues.listForRepo({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              labels: 'rebase-conflict',
              state: 'open'
            });
            
            if (issues.length === 0) {
              await github.rest.issues.create({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                title: 'Rebase conflict with upstream WezTerm',
                body: \`The nightly rebase on upstream WezTerm failed due to conflicts.
                
                **Action Required**: Manual resolution needed.
                
                \\\`\\\`\\\`bash
                git fetch upstream main
                git rebase upstream/main
                # Resolve conflicts
                git rebase --continue
                git push --force-with-lease
                \\\`\\\`\\\`
                \`,
                labels: ['rebase-conflict', 'urgent']
              });
            }
      
      - name: Run Tests After Rebase
        if: steps.rebase.outcome == 'success'
        run: |
          cargo build --features wa-integration
          cargo test --features wa-integration

  notify:
    needs: rebase
    if: failure()
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Send Notification
        run: |
          # Notify via email, Slack, Discord, etc.
          echo "Rebase failed - notification would be sent"
\`\`\`

### Handling Conflicts

When conflicts occur:
1. CI creates GitHub issue with "rebase-conflict" label
2. Human/agent reviews conflicting files
3. Usually conflicts are in files we modified (minimal set)
4. Resolve, test, push

### Minimizing Conflicts

To reduce conflict frequency:
1. Keep wa changes behind \`#[cfg(feature)]\` — no logic changes to upstream code
2. Use separate files where possible (mux/src/wa_events.rs)
3. Add wa code at end of functions, not middle
4. Avoid reformatting upstream code

## Alternative: Patch-Based Approach

Instead of maintaining a branch, maintain a patch series:

\`\`\`bash
# Generate patches
git format-patch upstream/main..feature/wa-integration -o patches/

# Apply patches to fresh clone
git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/wez/wezterm.git
cd wezterm
git am ../patches/*.patch
\`\`\`

Patches are more explicit about what we change, easier to review.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] GitHub Actions workflow created
- [ ] Nightly rebase runs automatically
- [ ] Conflicts create GitHub issues
- [ ] Notifications configured
- [ ] Post-rebase tests run
- [ ] Documentation for manual conflict resolution
- [ ] 30-day trial period: track conflict frequency

## Files to Create

- .github/workflows/rebase-upstream.yml
- docs/maintaining-wezterm-fork.md

## References

- GitHub Actions: https://docs.github.com/en/actions
- Git rebase: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-rebase

 {"id":"bd-27f3","title":"Add wa robot workflow status command","description":"# wa robot workflow status

## Current State (CLI Stub Done)
✅ CLI subcommand structure added
✅ JSON schema created (wa-robot-workflow-status.json)
❌ Implementation returns not-implemented error

## What Remains
Query workflow_executions table for execution state.

## Implementation
1. Accept execution_id parameter
2. Query workflow_executions + workflow_step_log tables
3. Return status, current_step, step_logs, timestamps, error

## Testing Requirements
- Unit: Returns execution status by ID
- Unit: Shows step progress accurately
- Unit: Error code stability (E_EXECUTION_NOT_FOUND)
- E2E: See bd-qvbz for lifecycle scenario

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Queries workflow_executions table
- [ ] Returns real-time step progress
- [ ] Includes step_logs array
- [ ] JSON validates against schema
- [ ] Unit + E2E tests pass with detailed logging

 {"id":"bd-2gce","title":"CLI/robot/MCP: annotate + label + triage","description":"## What
Expose annotation and triage operations through CLI and machine APIs.

## Why
Automation and external tools need structured access to annotations.

## How
- CLI: `wa events annotate`, `wa events label`, `wa events triage`
- Robot/MCP endpoints mirror CLI and reuse storage layer
- Ensure redaction and audit logging

- Commands update and fetch annotations deterministically

 {"id":"bd-37zz","title":"[EPIC] Ruleset profiles + pane bookmarks","description":"## Background

## Non-Goals

## Considerations
- Profiles must be safe to switch while watcher runs
- Bookmarks should not leak sensitive pane titles

 {"id":"bd-3gon","title":"Design WaEventSink trait and IPC protocol for native WezTerm integration","description":"## Overview

Design the interface between vendored WezTerm and wa. This defines the contract for how WezTerm will emit events to wa without Lua involvement.

## Deliverables

### 1. WaEventSink Trait Definition

\`\`\`rust
/// Trait for receiving events from WezTerm.
/// 
/// Implementations should be non-blocking and thread-safe.
/// WezTerm calls these methods from various threads.
pub trait WaEventSink: Send + Sync + 'static {
    /// Called when new output is received for a pane.
    /// 
    /// # Arguments
    /// * \`pane_id\` - The WezTerm pane ID
    /// * \`data\` - Raw bytes of terminal output
    fn on_pane_output(&self, pane_id: u64, data: &[u8]);
    
    /// Called when pane state changes (title, dimensions, alt-screen).
    /// 
    /// # Arguments
    /// * \`pane_id\` - The WezTerm pane ID
    /// * \`state\` - New state snapshot
    fn on_pane_state_change(&self, pane_id: u64, state: &WaPaneState);
    
    /// Called when a user-var (OSC 1337) is set.
    /// 
    /// # Arguments
    /// * \`pane_id\` - The WezTerm pane ID
    /// * \`name\` - Variable name
    /// * \`value\` - Variable value
    fn on_user_var_changed(&self, pane_id: u64, name: &str, value: &str);
    
    /// Called when a new pane is created.
    fn on_pane_created(&self, pane_id: u64, domain: &str, cwd: Option<&str>);
    
    /// Called when a pane is destroyed.
    fn on_pane_destroyed(&self, pane_id: u64);
}

/// Pane state snapshot for state change events.
pub struct WaPaneState {
    pub title: String,
    pub rows: u16,
    pub cols: u16,
    pub is_alt_screen: bool,
    pub cursor_row: u32,
    pub cursor_col: u32,
}
\`\`\`

### 2. IPC Protocol Design

Protocol for WezTerm→wa communication over Unix socket:

**Message Format**: JSON Lines (newline-delimited JSON)

\`\`\`json
{"type":"pane_output","pane_id":0,"data":"base64...","ts":1706123456789}
{"type":"state_change","pane_id":0,"state":{"title":"zsh","rows":24,"cols":80,"is_alt_screen":false},"ts":...}
{"type":"user_var","pane_id":0,"name":"wa-ready","value":"1","ts":...}
{"type":"pane_created","pane_id":1,"domain":"local","cwd":"/home/user","ts":...}
{"type":"pane_destroyed","pane_id":1,"ts":...}
\`\`\`

**Socket Location**: \`$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/wa/events.sock\` or \`/tmp/wa-$USER/events.sock\`

**Connection Model**: 
- WezTerm connects to wa socket on startup (if available)
- Reconnects with backoff if connection lost
- Events dropped silently if wa not running (non-blocking)

### 3. Configuration

WezTerm config addition:
\`\`\`lua
-- Optional: enable wa integration
config.wa_event_socket = "/tmp/wa-user/events.sock"
config.wa_event_filter = {
    pane_output = true,      -- can be high volume
    state_change = true,
    user_var = true,
    pane_lifecycle = true,
}
\`\`\`

Or via environment variable:
\`\`\`bash
export WEZTERM_WA_SOCKET="/tmp/wa/events.sock"
\`\`\`

### 4. Thread Safety Considerations

WezTerm is heavily multi-threaded:
- Terminal parsing happens on dedicated threads
- UI updates on main thread
- User-var parsing on PTY reader threads

WaEventSink must be \`Send + Sync\`:
- Use \`Arc<WaEventSink>\` for shared ownership
- Internal buffering with lock-free queues if needed
- Non-blocking sends (drop events rather than block)

### 5. Rate Limiting (Optional)

Consider rate limiting high-frequency events:
- pane_output: Could batch/throttle to reduce IPC overhead
- state_change: Already rate-limited by WezTerm's internal update frequency

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] WaEventSink trait defined with doc comments
- [ ] IPC protocol documented (message types, format)
- [ ] Socket location strategy defined
- [ ] Configuration options specified
- [ ] Thread safety requirements documented
- [ ] Rate limiting strategy decided
- [ ] Design reviewed by maintainer

## Files to Create

- docs/vendored-wezterm-design.md — comprehensive design doc
- crates/wa-core/src/wezterm_native.rs — trait definitions (stub)

## References

- WezTerm mux architecture: https://github.com/wez/wezterm/tree/main/mux
- Unix socket IPC: tokio UnixStream
- JSON Lines: https://jsonlines.org/","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:48:41.716149348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T23:09:15.472079327Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T23:09:15.471993056Z","close_reason":"Added vendored WezTerm native event design doc + WaEventSink trait stub","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3gon","depends_on_id":"bd-2xe4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3iax","title":"IPC config + socket lifecycle design","description":"## What
Define IPC config options and socket lifecycle management.

## Why
We need safe defaults and predictable file paths for local clients.

## How
- Config: enabled flag, socket path, permissions
- Lifecycle: create/remove socket, handle stale sockets

## Success Criteria
- Socket path is deterministic and safe
- Startup/teardown handles crashes cleanly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OrangeBear","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:24.280472849Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T06:08:46.985540899Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T06:08:46.985462523Z","close_reason":"Added IPC config/path/permissions + layout resolution; set socket permissions; updated hot-reload rules and tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3iax","depends_on_id":"bd-15ds","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:22Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":32,"issue_id":"bd-3iax","author":"OrangeBear","text":"Summary
- Added `ipc` config with `enabled`, `socket_path`, and `permissions` (default 0o600).
- Workspace layout now resolves IPC socket from config (absolute or relative to `.wa/`).
- Path normalization expands `~` for `ipc.socket_path`.
- Permission warnings now honor `ipc.permissions` and skip when disabled.
- IPC bind sets socket file permissions; stale sockets still removed on bind.
- Hot-reload marks IPC changes as restart-required.

Tests
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`

 {"id":"bd-3kzu","title":"TUI/Web surfaces for saved searches","description":"## What
Expose saved searches in TUI and web dashboards.

## Why
Operators should be able to view/trigger saved searches without memorizing CLI flags.

## How
- TUI: list saved searches, run on demand, toggle enabled
- Web: read-only list + last run status (no mutations yet)

## Risks
- Feature gating: keep behind tui/web feature flags
